login.subtitle: Smarter signals. Better decisions.

overview.subtitle: Here's your market overview for today.

overview.mustSellDesc: High risk, declining or AVOID signals

overview.brainNoData: Add tickers to your watchlist and run scans — the brain will track virtual trades automatically.

signals.title: Signals
signals.noSignalsDesc: Signals will appear here after a scan completes. Hit Scan Now to run one.
signals.noCrypto: No crypto signals yet. Hit Scan Now to run a scan that includes crypto.
signals.noEquity: No equity signals found with the current filters.
signals.scanTriggered: Scan started! Results will appear in ~60 seconds.

breakdown.title: Signal Breakdown
breakdown.subtitle: Plain-English read of what each indicator is saying
breakdown.empty: No notable signals firing on this ticker.

bb_upper.label: Bollinger upper @ {pct}%
bb_upper.what: Price is sitting against the top of its 2σ envelope
bb_upper.why: Statistically stretched — most reversions happen from here

bb_lower.label: Bollinger lower @ {pct}%
bb_lower.what: Price is pinned to the bottom of its 2σ envelope
bb_lower.why: Often where bottoms form — watch for a reversal candle

rsi_overbought.label: RSI {rsi} (overbought)
rsi_overbought.what: Buyers have been in control for too long
rsi_overbought.why: Momentum exhaustion typically precedes a pullback

rsi_oversold.label: RSI {rsi} (oversold)
rsi_oversold.what: Sellers have pushed it well below the mean
rsi_oversold.why: Can mean-revert OR keep falling — don't catch a knife

rsi_sweet_spot.label: RSI {rsi} (sweet spot)
rsi_sweet_spot.why: Backtest shows the highest win rates between 50–65

macd_bullish.label: MACD bullish
macd_bullish.what: MACD line above signal, histogram rising
macd_bullish.why: Trend is confirming itself — common entry trigger

macd_bearish_divergence.label: MACD bearish divergence
macd_bearish_divergence.what: MACD histogram is fading while price is still rising
macd_bearish_divergence.why: Classic late-cycle warning — momentum is leaving the move

volume_surge.label: Volume surge ({z}σ)
volume_surge.what: Today's volume is well above the 30-day average
volume_surge.why: Conviction behind the move — institutional footprint

volume_dry.label: Volume drying up ({z}σ)
volume_dry.why: Lack of buyers makes any move fragile and easy to reverse

strong_trend.label: Strong trend (ADX {adx})
strong_trend.what: ADX above 25 indicates a directional, persistent trend
strong_trend.why: Trends tend to continue — momentum entries work better here

golden_cross.label: Golden cross
golden_cross.why: Long-term uptrend confirmation — a structural buy signal

death_cross.label: Death cross
death_cross.why: Long-term downtrend confirmation — a structural risk signal

pe_rich.label: P/E {pe} (rich)
pe_rich.what: Trading at a premium to its earnings
pe_rich.why: Little margin for an earnings miss — disappointment hurts more

pe_cheap.label: P/E {pe} (cheap)
pe_cheap.what: Trading at a discount to its earnings
pe_cheap.why: Margin of safety — downside is partially priced in

eps_growth_strong.label: Strong EPS growth
eps_growth_strong.what: Earnings are growing at a healthy clip year over year
eps_growth_strong.why: Quality compounder — earnings growth drives long-term returns

eps_growth_negative.label: EPS shrinking
eps_growth_negative.what: Earnings are declining year over year

revenue_growth_strong.label: Strong revenue growth
revenue_growth_strong.what: Top-line is expanding at a healthy clip
revenue_growth_strong.why: Demand is real — gives the earnings story a foundation

high_debt.label: High debt/equity ({de}%)
high_debt.what: Significant leverage on the balance sheet
high_debt.why: Higher fragility in a downturn — interest costs eat margins

dividend_attractive.label: Attractive dividend yield
dividend_attractive.what: Pays a meaningful dividend relative to its price
dividend_attractive.why: Income cushion while you wait for the thesis to play out

rr_strong.label: Risk/reward {rr}x (strong)
rr_strong.what: Upside to target is at least 3× the distance to stop loss
rr_strong.why: Asymmetric setup — small losses, big wins when right

rr_weak.label: Risk/reward {rr}x (weak)
rr_weak.what: Below the 2.0× floor the brain wants for new entries
rr_weak.why: Even if it works, the payoff doesn't justify the stop distance

regime_volatile.label: Volatile market regime
regime_volatile.what: VIX 20–30 or S&P caught between SMA50 and SMA200
regime_volatile.why: Brain is in caution mode — score thresholds raised, sizing reduced

regime_crisis.label: Crisis market regime
regime_crisis.what: VIX above 30 or S&P below SMA200
regime_crisis.why: Brain pauses high-risk picks entirely; defensive positioning only

iv_complacency.label: Options IV {pct} percentile
iv_complacency.why: Market is asleep on this name — textbook complacency tell

iv_panic.label: Options IV {pct} percentile
iv_panic.why: Market is panicking — premium-selling opportunity, but risk is real

near_52w_high.label: Near 52-week high
near_52w_high.why: Limited overhead room before resistance kicks in

near_52w_low.label: Near 52-week low
near_52w_low.why: Possibly oversold or in a downtrend — context matters

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.pingTooltip: Click to send a test ping
integrations.supabaseDesc: PostgreSQL database for signals, rules, positions, and audit logs.
integrations.telegramDesc: Bot for OTP login, GEM alerts, scan digests, and watchlist notifications.
integrations.claudeDesc: Primary AI synthesis engine. Produces signal reasoning, target prices, and risk analysis.
integrations.geminiDesc: Free tier fallback for synthesis and sentiment. 1,500 req/day on 2.0-flash.
integrations.grokDesc: Real-time X/Twitter sentiment analysis. Only provider with live social media access.
integrations.watchdogDesc: Monitors open brain positions every 15 min during market hours. Checks prices and sentiment to protect trades.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Signa and how to use it effectively.
howItWorks.goalDesc: Signa is an AI-powered investment signal engine designed for self-directed investors. It automatically scans over 1,000 stocks and cryptocurrencies across the TSX (Toronto Stock Exchange), NYSE, NASDAQ, and major crypto markets — four times every trading day. Instead of spending hours researching, you get clear BUY, HOLD, SELL, or AVOID signals backed by real data and AI analysis.
howItWorks.whoDesc: Signa is built for Canadian investors who use platforms like Wealthsimple, Questrade, or Interactive Brokers. It supports TFSA, RRSP, and taxable accounts. Whether you prefer safe dividend stocks or high-growth momentum plays, Signa classifies every signal into the right bucket for your strategy.
howItWorks.scanDesc: Every trading day (Monday to Friday), Signa runs 5 automated scans at key market moments:
howItWorks.scan1: 6:00 AM ET — Pre-market scan: catches overnight news, earnings, and pre-market movers before the bell rings.
howItWorks.scan2: 10:00 AM ET — Market open scan: confirms or adjusts signals after the first 30 minutes of trading when real volume data is available.
howItWorks.scan2b: 12:00 PM ET — Midday scan: fills the gap between market open and pre-close. Catches morning reversals, lunch-hour moves, and ensures no position goes unchecked for more than 2 hours during trading.
howItWorks.scan3: 3:00 PM ET — Pre-close scan: identifies end-of-day momentum shifts and last-minute opportunities before the close.
howItWorks.scan4: 4:30 PM ET — After-close scan: full analysis of the complete trading day including after-hours moves.
howItWorks.scanNote: On weekends and holidays, scheduled scans are paused. Crypto markets trade 24/7, so crypto signals may still update.
howItWorks.scanOnDemandDesc: You don't have to wait for the next scheduled scan. On the Signals page, hit the "Scan Now" button to trigger an immediate scan. This runs the full analysis pipeline — pre-filter, technicals, fundamentals, macro, sentiment, and AI synthesis — and results appear in about 60 seconds. On-demand scans don't affect the regular schedule; the next scheduled scan will still run at its normal time.
howItWorks.pipelineDesc: Each scan follows a rigorous multi-step process:
howItWorks.discoveryDesc: Before each scan, Signa queries Yahoo Finance screeners (most active, day gainers, undervalued large caps, growth tech) to find trending stocks not in the core universe of ~260 tickers. These discovered tickers go through the same analysis pipeline. This is how the brain finds gems it wouldn't see in a fixed list — if a stock is making unusual moves today, the brain will evaluate it.
howItWorks.step1Desc: From 270+ tickers, Signa filters down to the top 50 candidates based on trading volume (minimum 200K shares), price (minimum $1), and absolute day change (minimum 1%). Candidates are sorted by how much they moved — the most active tickers get priority. This means a ticker on your watchlist may not appear in every scan if it had a quiet day. Up to 5 slots are reserved for crypto so equities don't crowd them out.
howItWorks.step2Desc: For each candidate, Signa computes 15+ technical indicators including RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, moving averages (SMA 20/50/200), volume trends, and momentum scores using real-time price data from Yahoo Finance.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Key fundamentals are evaluated: P/E ratio, EPS growth, dividend yield, profit margins, debt ratios, and market cap. This helps distinguish between genuinely strong companies and overhyped momentum plays.
howItWorks.step4Desc: Signa pulls the latest macroeconomic data from FRED (Federal Reserve): interest rates, CPI inflation, VIX volatility index, and Fed policy trends. A hostile macro environment can block otherwise strong signals.
howItWorks.step5Desc: Signa gathers sentiment from multiple sources — Grok AI analyzes X/Twitter for real-time market mood, breaking news, and social momentum, while options flow data (put/call ratios, IV percentile, unusual volume) provides institutional-level insight. When social sentiment and options flow agree, confidence increases. When they conflict, the signal is flagged as uncertain for deeper AI analysis. Sentiment only runs for High Risk tickers (35% of the score). Safe Income tickers skip it (10% weight) to save API costs.
howItWorks.step6Desc: Claude AI receives all the data — technicals, fundamentals, macro, sentiment, and options flow — and produces a final assessment: action (BUY/HOLD/SELL/AVOID), confidence level, target price, stop loss, risk/reward ratio, and a plain-English reasoning explaining the thesis.
howItWorks.step7Desc: Each signal receives a score from 0-100. Scores above 75 are BUY signals. The signal is classified into a bucket (Safe Income or High Risk) based on the ticker's characteristics.
howItWorks.buyDesc: BUY (score 75+) — Strong opportunity. The data, AI analysis, and market conditions all align favorably. Consider entering a position.
howItWorks.holdDesc: HOLD (score 50-74) — Decent outlook but not compelling enough for a new entry. If you already own it, keep watching.
howItWorks.avoidDesc: AVOID (score below 50) — Negative signals outweigh positives. Risk/reward is unfavorable right now.
howItWorks.sellDesc: SELL — Active deterioration detected. If you hold this position, consider exiting.
howItWorks.confirmedDesc: CONFIRMED — Multiple data sources agree. This is a high-conviction signal.
howItWorks.weakDesc: WEAKENING — The signal was previously stronger. Conditions are deteriorating.
howItWorks.upgradedDesc: UPGRADED — Signal improved since last scan. Momentum is building.
howItWorks.cancelledDesc: CANCELLED — Previous signal is no longer valid. Conditions changed significantly.
howItWorks.gemsDesc: GEMs are the highest-quality signals Signa can produce. A signal becomes a GEM when ALL of these conditions are met simultaneously:
howItWorks.gem2: Bullish sentiment confirmed by Grok AI
howItWorks.gem3: A clear catalyst within the next 30 days (earnings, product launch, etc.)
howItWorks.gem5: No red flags or blockers detected
howItWorks.gemsNote: GEMs are rare — typically 0-3 per day. When one appears, you receive an instant alert on Telegram with the full analysis. These are the signals worth paying the most attention to.
howItWorks.safeDesc: Stable, dividend-paying stocks from established companies. Think Canadian banks (RY, TD, BMO), utilities (FTS, EMA), REITs, and blue-chip dividend aristocrats. Scored heavily on dividend reliability, fundamental health, and macro conditions. Lower risk, steady returns. Ideal for TFSA and RRSP accounts.
howItWorks.riskDesc: Growth stocks, tech momentum, biotech, cannabis, crypto, and small caps. Think SHOP, NVDA, BTC, TSLA. Scored heavily on sentiment, catalysts, and technical momentum. Higher potential returns but more volatile. Best for taxable accounts or small allocations.
howItWorks.watchlistDesc: Add any ticker to your watchlist to track it across scans. When a watchlisted ticker generates a signal, you'll see it highlighted. Use the watchlist to keep an eye on stocks you're interested in but haven't committed to yet.
howItWorks.telegramDesc: Signa sends real-time alerts to your Telegram when important events happen:
howItWorks.tg1: GEM alerts — instant notification with full analysis when a rare high-quality signal is detected
howItWorks.tg2: Position alerts — stop loss warnings, target price reached, significant P&L changes
howItWorks.tg3: Scan digests — summary of each completed scan
howItWorks.telegramNote: You can test the Telegram connection anytime by clicking the Telegram row in the Integrations panel on the sidebar.
howItWorks.themesDesc: Signa offers 6 visual themes (Apple Stocks, Robinhood, Wealthsimple, Bloomberg, Webull, E*Trade) in both light and dark modes. Switch themes using the palette icon in the top navigation. You can also switch between English and Portuguese using the language toggle.
howItWorks.tip1: Check the Overview page in the morning before market open for the pre-market scan results.
howItWorks.tip2: Pay attention to GEMs — they're rare but have the highest conviction.
howItWorks.tip3: Use the score filter on the Signals page to focus on 70+ or 80+ signals only.
howItWorks.tip4: Compare the Safe Income and High Risk buckets to balance your portfolio strategy.
howItWorks.tip5: Add tickers you're watching to the Watchlist so you never miss a signal on them.
howItWorks.tip6: If a signal status changes from CONFIRMED to WEAKENING, re-evaluate your position.
howItWorks.riskLevelsDesc: On the Overview page, the Safe to Buy and Consider Selling sections show each signal with a risk level badge:
howItWorks.riskLow: Low risk — Safe Income BUY signal with score 75+, or any BUY with score 70+ and risk/reward ratio 2:1 or better. These are the safest opportunities.
howItWorks.riskMed: Medium risk — BUY signal with score 60-69. Decent opportunity but not as strong. Use caution and smaller position sizes.
howItWorks.riskHigh: High risk — Score below 60, SELL, AVOID, or WEAKENING signals. These require attention — consider exiting or avoiding new positions.
howItWorks.detailDesc: Click 'View full analysis' on any signal card (or click a ticker in Quick Actions) to see the full detail page. This includes: current price, latest signal with full reasoning, scoring breakdown showing which factors (sentiment, technicals, fundamentals, macro) drove the score, fundamental data (P/E, EPS growth, dividend yield, market cap), and a complete signal history for that ticker.
howItWorks.scoringDesc: Each signal gets a score from 0 to 100. The score is calculated differently depending on the bucket:
howItWorks.scoringSafe: Safe Income model: 35% dividend reliability, 30% fundamental health, 25% macro conditions, 10% sentiment. This emphasizes stability — a stock needs solid fundamentals and a favorable macro environment.
howItWorks.scoringRisk: High Risk model: 35% X/Twitter sentiment, 30% catalyst presence, 25% technical momentum, 10% fundamentals. This emphasizes momentum — a stock needs social buzz, upcoming catalysts, and strong technical signals.
howItWorks.scoringNote: The scoring model was validated through a 6-month backtest (Oct 2024 - Apr 2025) across 30 tickers. Key findings: RSI 50-65 is the sweet spot, momentum above 5% is a trap, and scores above 72 can indicate overbought conditions.
howItWorks.brainRulesIntro: The brain is the autonomous decision engine that auto-buys and auto-sells virtual positions to track its own performance. It operates under strict rules to make sure every trade is high-conviction and realistic.
howItWorks.brainTieredDesc: The brain only auto-buys signals that meet one of three trust tiers. This prevents it from acting on noisy or incomplete data:
howItWorks.brainTier1: Tier 1 (full position): AI synthesis succeeded with confidence 50+ and score 72+. Highest conviction — full Kelly position size. This is the default for most brain picks.
howItWorks.brainTier2: Tier 2 (half position): AI ran but had low confidence (<50), score 80+, no blockers. The higher score bar compensates for lower AI conviction. Half position size to manage risk.
howItWorks.brainTier3: Tier 3 (half position): AI never ran (tech-only, below top 15 by pre-score), score 82+, AND requires 3 of 4 technical confirmations: RSI in sweet spot 50-65, MACD histogram positive, volume z-score 1.0-2.5, and within 30% of SMA200. Hostile macro environment also blocks Tier 3 (extra safety since the tech-only path skips the regular blocker checks). Strict criteria substitute for missing AI validation.
howItWorks.brainTierFailed: Failed AI is never auto-bought. When synthesis errors out, the ticker is added to a retry queue and re-attempted on the next scan. This prevents transient API issues from causing permanent signal loss.
howItWorks.brainMarketHoursDesc: The brain only auto-buys and auto-sells equities when US markets are open (Monday-Friday, 9:30am-4:00pm ET). Trading outside hours produces unrealistic fill prices, so the brain waits. Crypto is exempt and trades 24/7. The watchdog also respects these hours — equity positions aren't monitored or force-closed during pre-market or after-hours.
howItWorks.brainPreMarketReviewDesc: When a held equity gets a SELL or AVOID signal during a pre-market scan (e.g., the 6am scan), the brain can't actually sell. Instead, it flags the position for review and sends an instant Telegram alert: "⚠ Brain Flagged for Review".
howItWorks.brainReviewFlow1: At the first scan after market open (9:30am+ ET), the brain re-evaluates each flagged position with fresh data.
howItWorks.brainReviewFlow2: If the signal is still SELL/AVOID → executes the sell immediately and clears the flag.
howItWorks.brainReviewFlow3: If the signal recovered to BUY/HOLD → clears the flag, keeps the position, and sends a "✅ Brain Review Cleared" alert.
howItWorks.brainReviewFlow4: Pre-market noise gets a second chance to recover, while real deterioration gets sold within minutes of the open. You're never surprised — you get a Telegram alert at 6am AND at 9:30am.
howItWorks.brainTelegramCommandsDesc: If you want to override the brain's pre-market review decision, you can send commands directly from Telegram (requires the webhook to be configured in Settings):
howItWorks.brainCmd1: /review — list all positions currently flagged for review
howItWorks.brainCmd2: /forcesell TICKER — confirm sell at the next market open, even if the signal recovers
howItWorks.brainCmd3: /keep TICKER — clear the review flag and keep the position
howItWorks.brainAIChainDesc: AI synthesis is critical to the brain's decisions, so Signa has multiple layers of redundancy:
howItWorks.brainAIChain1: Claude Code CLI (free, runs locally) is tried first with up to 3 retries (2s, 4s, 8s backoff) for transient failures like timeouts or empty responses.
howItWorks.brainAIChain2: If Claude Local exhausts retries, the paid Claude API is tried next.
howItWorks.brainAIChain3: If Claude API fails or is over budget, Gemini (free tier) is tried as the final fallback.
howItWorks.brainAIChain4: If ALL providers fail for a ticker, it's added to the AI retry queue and re-attempted on the next scan. The brain never auto-buys signals where AI failed — it waits for a successful synthesis.
howItWorks.brainAlertsDesc: The brain monitors itself and alerts you when something is off:
howItWorks.brainAlert1: Budget thresholds — Telegram alert when monthly AI spending crosses 70%, 90%, or 100% of the per-provider limit. Lets you top up before the brain goes blind.
howItWorks.brainAlert2: AI failure rate — if more than 50% of AI candidates in a single scan fail synthesis, you get an alert with the failure breakdown. Catches systemic issues (CLI dead, API key revoked) before the brain operates blind for hours.
howItWorks.brainAlert3: Tier breakdown widget on the Overview page shows win rate per tier so you can validate whether T2/T3 is profitable and tune the model.
howItWorks.brainScoreDropGuardDesc: If a held position's score suddenly drops by 25+ points to below 50, the brain refuses to auto-sell. This usually means the AI failed to analyze the ticker on this scan (tech-only fallback) rather than a real deterioration. The brain waits for the next scan to confirm. The watchdog will still force-close the position within 15 minutes if real danger persists. The /forcesell command bypasses this guard for explicit user overrides.
howItWorks.blockersDesc: Some conditions automatically block a BUY signal regardless of score:
howItWorks.blocker1: RSI above 75 — the stock is likely overbought and due for a pullback
howItWorks.blocker2: Hostile macro environment — rising rates + high inflation + high VIX
howItWorks.blocker3: Extremely low volume — less than 200K daily average, too illiquid
howItWorks.blocker4: Fraud or integrity red flags detected in fundamental data
howItWorks.priceDisplayDesc: Each signal card shows the current price and a secondary value underneath. This can be one of two things:
howItWorks.priceDisplay1: Percentage change (e.g. +1.09%) — shown when live price data is available from Yahoo Finance. This is the day's price change compared to yesterday's close.
howItWorks.priceDisplay2: Score (e.g. 44/100) — shown when live price data is unavailable (the stock may be delisted, illiquid, or the data feed timed out). The score is still valid — it just means the price couldn't be fetched at that moment.
howItWorks.priceDisplayNote: The $0.00 price means no current price data was found. The signal's price_at_signal field still shows what the price was when the scan ran.
howItWorks.readingCardTitle: How to Read a Signal Card
howItWorks.readingCardDesc: Each signal card is packed with information. Here's what each element means:
howItWorks.readingCard1: Top left: Ticker symbol, exchange (TSX/NYSE/NASDAQ/Crypto), and a star icon to add/remove from watchlist.
howItWorks.readingCard2: Top right: Current price (or price at signal time if live data unavailable) and daily change percentage (or score/100 fallback).
howItWorks.readingCard3: Score ring: Visual 0-100 score. Green (80+) = strong, orange (60-79) = moderate, red (below 60) = weak.
howItWorks.readingCard4: Target / Stop Loss / Risk Reward: The AI's recommended exit points and risk/reward ratio.
howItWorks.readingCard5: Badges: Action (BUY/HOLD/SELL/AVOID), Status (CONFIRMED/WEAKENING/UPGRADED/CANCELLED), and style (MOMENTUM/CONTRARIAN if detected).
howItWorks.readingCard6: Probability chip: Shows the percentage chance of beating SPY over 20 days (e.g., '62% vs SPY'). Only appears when probability exceeds 50%. Computed from 18 months of backtest data.
howItWorks.readingCard7: Discovered badge: Appears when the ticker was found by the Yahoo Finance screener discovery layer, not from the core universe.
howItWorks.readingCard8: Expanded view: Click the card to see the full reasoning, scoring breakdown, sentiment data, and a link to the full detail page.
howItWorks.aiProvidersDesc: Signa uses multiple AI providers for different tasks. You can configure the priority order in Settings:
howItWorks.aiProvider1: Claude (Anthropic) — Primary synthesis engine. Takes all data (technicals, fundamentals, macro, sentiment) and produces the final BUY/HOLD/SELL/AVOID signal with reasoning, target price, and stop loss. Most sophisticated analysis but requires paid credits.
howItWorks.aiProvider2: Gemini (Google) — Free tier fallback for both synthesis and sentiment. Uses Gemini 2.0 Flash with 1,500 free requests/day. Good quality analysis, slightly less nuanced than Claude but excellent for testing and low-volume use.
howItWorks.aiProvider3: Grok (xAI) — Sentiment specialist with real-time X/Twitter access. The only provider that can read live social media posts. Critical for accurate sentiment scoring and catching breaking news. Requires paid credits.
howItWorks.aiProviderNote: If the primary provider fails (no credits, rate limited, API down), Signa automatically tries the next provider in your configured order. The signal card shows which provider was used.
howItWorks.cryptoDesc: Signa handles crypto and traditional stocks differently:
howItWorks.crypto1: Crypto tickers end in -USD (e.g., BTC-USD, ETH-USD, SOL-USD) and are always classified as HIGH_RISK bucket.
howItWorks.crypto2: Crypto markets trade 24/7 — signals can update even on weekends when equity markets are closed.
howItWorks.crypto3: The pre-filter reserves at least 5 slots for crypto tickers so they don't get crowded out by higher-volume equities.
howItWorks.crypto4: Technical indicators work the same way, but fundamental data (P/E, dividends) is not available for crypto. Scoring relies more heavily on sentiment and momentum.
howItWorks.accountsDesc: Signa is designed for Canadian investors. Here's how signals map to account types:
howItWorks.account1: TFSA (Tax-Free Savings Account) — Best for Safe Income signals. Dividends and capital gains are tax-free. Focus on stable dividend payers like Canadian banks and utilities.
howItWorks.account2: RRSP (Registered Retirement Savings Plan) — Good for both buckets. Contributions are tax-deductible, growth is tax-deferred. US dividend stocks avoid the 15% withholding tax when held in RRSP.
howItWorks.account3: Taxable Account — Best for High Risk signals. Capital gains are taxed at 50% inclusion rate. Use this for speculative plays, crypto, and momentum trades where you might need quick exits.
howItWorks.accountBadgesDesc: Each signal card shows a recommended Canadian account type: TFSA (Tax-Free Savings Account) for Safe Income signals — dividends and stable stocks where gains are tax-free. RRSP (Registered Retirement Savings Plan) for High Risk signals — active trading is more protected from CRA business income classification in RRSP. TAXABLE for signals that don't fit either registered account, or when your TFSA/RRSP contribution room is full. This is guidance based on Canadian tax rules, not financial advice — consult a tax professional.
howItWorks.watchlistAlertsDesc: When you add a ticker to your watchlist, Signa monitors it across every scan. If a watchlisted ticker receives a SELL or AVOID signal, you get an instant Telegram alert with the full analysis so you can take action immediately. This is especially useful for tickers you own but haven't logged as positions yet.
howItWorks.brainDesc: Signa's signal engine is powered by a set of investment rules and market knowledge stored in a database. These rules define scoring weights, blocker conditions, GEM criteria, and sell triggers. Advanced users can view and edit these rules through the Brain Editor (accessible from the sidebar). The Brain Editor requires a separate Telegram verification for security — this is a power user tool that directly affects how signals are generated.
howItWorks.integrationsPageDesc: The Integrations page shows the health status of every service Signa depends on — Supabase, Telegram, Claude AI, Gemini AI, Grok AI, and the Scheduler. Each card displays the current status, model version (for AI providers), and any error details. You can send a test message to Telegram directly from this page to verify the connection is working.
howItWorks.logsPageDesc: The Logs page streams live application logs over WebSocket. You can filter by log level (INFO, WARN, ERROR) and search for specific text. Access requires OTP verification via Telegram for security — sessions last 15 minutes before requiring re-authentication.
howItWorks.selfLearningDesc: The Suggestions tab in the Brain Editor lets Signa's AI analyze your trade outcomes over the past 7 days and propose rule changes to improve future signals. Each suggestion shows a confidence score and win rate impact. You can approve, reject, or apply suggestions directly to the brain rules.
howItWorks.quickActionsDesc: The Overview page features two Quick Action panels: 'Safe to Buy' shows low-risk, high-conviction BUY signals you can act on immediately, while 'Consider Selling' highlights declining or high-risk positions that may need attention. Each entry shows the ticker, score, and risk level badge.
howItWorks.twoPassDesc: Signa uses a two-pass scanning optimization: the top 15 candidates by volume/price action receive full AI analysis (reasoning, sentiment, target prices), while the remaining 35 get tech-only scoring. This balances analysis depth with API cost. You can configure these thresholds in Settings under the Scanning section.
howItWorks.regimeBadgesDesc: Each signal may carry a market regime tag — TRENDING, VOLATILE, or CRISIS — reflecting the current macro environment. In TRENDING mode, full signals are generated with normal Kelly sizing. In VOLATILE mode, High Risk scores are reduced 15% and Kelly is halved. In CRISIS mode, High Risk signals are paused entirely and only Safe Income dividends are shown.
howItWorks.kellyDesc: Signa uses fractional Kelly criterion (25% of full Kelly) to suggest position sizes, adjusted by market regime. In normal conditions, Kelly sizing applies fully. In volatile markets, Kelly is halved. In crisis mode, Kelly is capped at 5%. This prevents overexposure during uncertain conditions while still optimizing for expected returns.
howItWorks.settingsDesc: The Settings page lets you configure AI provider priority (drag to reorder), scanning parameters (pre-filter candidates, AI candidates), toggle AI analysis on/off for cost control, switch between 6 visual themes in light/dark mode, change language (English/Portuguese), and log out. Changes to AI config are saved to the server immediately.
howItWorks.watchlistStarDesc: On every signal card and ticker detail page, you'll see a star icon next to the ticker symbol. A filled star means the ticker is in your watchlist; an outlined star means it's not. Tap the star to toggle — adding or removing the ticker from your watchlist instantly.
howItWorks.dualSignalDesc: Signa generates two types of BUY signals that catch different opportunities. MOMENTUM signals identify stocks already rising with strong technical indicators — you're riding an existing trend. CONTRARIAN signals identify beaten-down stocks showing early recovery signs (below SMA200, oversold RSI, volume picking up, MACD turning positive). Contrarian signals require 3 of 4 conditions and are validated by a 2-year backtest at 59.2% win rate (66.4% for Safe Income). Each signal card shows a badge: MOMENTUM (green) or CONTRARIAN (orange). You can filter by signal style on the Signals page.
howItWorks.priceOutlookDesc: When you expand a signal card, the Price Outlook section shows three values side by side: Stop Loss (red, with percentage downside), Current Price (center), and Target Price (green, with percentage upside). This makes the risk/reward immediately visual — for example, Current $10, Target $12 (+20%), Stop $9 (-10%) means you could gain twice what you risk. Target and stop loss are set by the AI during synthesis based on technical levels and fundamental analysis.
howItWorks.scoreThresholdsDesc: In Settings, you can configure separate BUY thresholds for Safe Income and High Risk signals. Each slider shows the backtest-validated win rate at that threshold. For example, Safe Income at 62+ has a 60.6% win rate over 2 years, while lowering to 60+ increases signals but maintains 60.9% accuracy. High Risk signals max out around 53% with the scorer alone — the AI layer adds the extra edge. The HOLD/AVOID cutoff determines when a signal is too weak to consider at all.
howItWorks.otpPasteDesc: When logging in or unlocking the Brain Editor, you can paste all 6 digits of your OTP code at once. Copy the code from your Telegram message and paste it into the first input box — all 6 fields will auto-fill. This works on both desktop and mobile devices.
howItWorks.dailyRoutineDesc: Here is how to use Signa every day to stay on top of the market:
howItWorks.daily1Desc: Open the dashboard and check the overview — the stats bar shows GEMs, win rate, tickers scanned, and today's AI cost.
howItWorks.daily2Desc: Look at "Safe to Buy" — these are the brain's top BUY signals for Safe Income stocks. The higher the score, the more confident the signal.
howItWorks.daily3Desc: Check "Consider Selling" — if you own any of these tickers, review them. They have AVOID signals.
howItWorks.daily4Desc: Check your Watchlist — if any watchlisted ticker gets a SELL or AVOID signal, you'll get a Telegram alert immediately.
howItWorks.daily5Desc: Go to the Signals page for the full list — use filters (asset type, market Canada/US, signal style, bucket, action, score) to find what you're looking for. The Canada/US market filter is useful when you want to focus on TSX-only or US-only opportunities.
howItWorks.daily6Desc: Tap a ticker to see the full detail — read the Brain Summary, check target price vs current price, and review the AI reasoning.
howItWorks.daily7Desc: Check Telegram for urgent alerts — GEM alerts, watchlist SELL notifications, and scan digests are sent automatically.
howItWorks.whatShouldBuyDesc: Look for these conditions before entering a position:
howItWorks.shouldBuy1: Score 70+ — the higher the better. Minimum is 62 for Safe Income, 65 for High Risk.
howItWorks.shouldBuy2: BUY + CONFIRMED status — means multiple scans agree on this recommendation, not a one-time blip.
howItWorks.shouldBuy3: Risk/Reward above 2x — you want at least $2 of upside for every $1 you risk. Above 3x is excellent.
howItWorks.shouldBuy4: Target and Stop Loss both filled — if the AI shows "--" for target/stop, it means low conviction. Skip it.
howItWorks.shouldBuy5: MOMENTUM or CONTRARIAN badge — MOMENTUM means the trend is in your favor. CONTRARIAN means the stock is beaten down but showing reversal signs.
howItWorks.shouldBuy6: Account badge (TFSA/RRSP/TAXABLE) — tells you which Canadian account type is best for tax efficiency.
howItWorks.whatShouldNotBuyTitle: What Should Make You NOT Buy
howItWorks.whatShouldNotBuyDesc: Avoid entering a position when you see any of these red flags:
howItWorks.shouldNotBuy1: Score below 65 — not enough conviction from the brain.
howItWorks.shouldNotBuy2: No target or stop loss ("--") — the AI couldn't determine price targets, meaning weak conviction.
howItWorks.shouldNotBuy3: AVOID or HOLD action — the brain is saying wait or stay away.
howItWorks.shouldNotBuy4: VOLATILE or CRISIS market regime — be more selective, use smaller position sizes. In CRISIS mode, only Safe Income dividend plays are active.
howItWorks.shouldNotBuy5: WEAKENING or CANCELLED status — the signal was stronger before but is now deteriorating.
howItWorks.shouldNotBuy6: Red flags in the AI reasoning — watch for words like "overbought", "declining earnings", "fraud", or "overvalued".
howItWorks.dashboardCardsDesc: Here is what each element on the Overview dashboard means:
howItWorks.dashCard1: GEMs — Count of today's high-conviction signals that meet ALL 5 conditions (score 85+, sentiment 80%+, catalyst within 30 days, R/R 3.0+, no red flags). GEMs are rare and very actionable.
howItWorks.dashCard2: Win Rate — Historical accuracy over the last 30 days. Improves as the self-learning loop analyzes trade outcomes.
howItWorks.dashCard3: Tickers Scanned — Total tickers processed today across all scans. The universe has 188+ tickers across TSX, NYSE, NASDAQ, and crypto.
howItWorks.dashCard4: AI Cost — Today's real spending on Claude (synthesis) and Grok (sentiment). Budget limits prevent overspending. Gemini is free tier.
howItWorks.dashCard5: Safe to Buy — Top 5 BUY signals for Safe Income bucket, sorted by score. Low risk, dividend-paying stocks.
howItWorks.dashCard6: Consider Selling — Top 5 AVOID/SELL signals for High Risk bucket. Crypto and volatile stocks showing weakness.
howItWorks.glossaryDesc: Common abbreviations and terms used throughout Signa:

glossary.meaning: One-Time Password — a 6-digit verification code sent to your Telegram for login and security actions.
JSON Web Token — the authentication token that keeps you logged in. Stored in your browser.
Two-Factor Authentication — a security method requiring two steps to verify identity (password + Telegram code).
Golden Entry Moment — Signa's highest-conviction signal. Requires score 85+, bullish sentiment, catalyst, and 3:1+ risk/reward.
Relative Strength Index — a momentum indicator (0-100). Above 70 = overbought, below 30 = oversold. Signa's sweet spot: 50-65.
Moving Average Convergence Divergence — a trend-following indicator that shows momentum direction and strength.
Simple Moving Average — the average price over N days. SMA 50 (short-term trend) and SMA 200 (long-term trend) are used.
Average True Range — measures price volatility over 14 days. Higher ATR = more volatile stock.
Price-to-Earnings Ratio — stock price divided by earnings per share. Lower P/E may indicate undervaluation.
Earnings Per Share — company profit divided by number of shares. Growth in EPS is a positive fundamental signal.
Risk/Reward Ratio — potential upside divided by potential downside. A 3:1 R/R means you could gain $3 for every $1 risked.
Volatility Index — measures expected market volatility. Below 20 = calm, 20-30 = elevated, above 30 = crisis.
Federal Reserve Economic Data — source for US macro data (interest rates, inflation, unemployment).
Consumer Price Index — measures inflation. Rising CPI means prices are increasing.
Tax-Free Savings Account — Canadian account where investment gains are tax-free. Best for Safe Income signals.
Registered Retirement Savings Plan — Canadian account with tax-deferred growth. Best for High Risk signals.
Toronto Stock Exchange — Canada's main stock exchange. Tickers end in .TO (e.g., SHOP.TO, RY.TO).
New York Stock Exchange — largest US stock exchange (e.g., AAPL, JPM).
National Association of Securities Dealers Automated Quotations — US tech-heavy exchange (e.g., NVDA, TSLA).
Post-Earnings Announcement Drift — stocks tend to continue moving in the direction of an earnings surprise for days after the report.
Kelly Criterion — a mathematical formula for optimal position sizing based on win rate and risk/reward ratio.
Application Programming Interface — how Signa communicates with external services (AI providers, data feeds, Telegram).
Artificial Intelligence — Claude, Gemini, and Grok analyze data and produce investment signals.
Exchange-Traded Fund — a basket of stocks traded as a single security (e.g., XIU.TO tracks the TSX).
Real Estate Investment Trust — companies that own income-producing real estate. Often classified as Safe Income.
Profit and Loss — your gain or loss on a position, shown as both dollar amount and percentage.

howItWorks.telegramMsgsDesc: Signa sends different types of messages via Telegram depending on what happens during a scan. Each message includes a timestamp in ET timezone.
howItWorks.tgMsg1: GEM Alert — Sent immediately when a signal meets all 5 GEM conditions (score 85+, sentiment 80%+, catalyst within 30 days, R/R 3.0+, no red flags). Includes price, target, stop loss, risk/reward, and AI reasoning.
howItWorks.tgMsg2: Watchlist Alert — Sent when a ticker on your watchlist receives a SELL or AVOID signal. Shows score, price, bucket (Safe Income or High Risk), target/stop if available, and analysis summary.
howItWorks.tgMsg3: Scan Digest — Sent after Pre-Market and After-Close scans. Summarizes total signals, BUY count, GEM count, and the top 3 signals by score.
howItWorks.tgMsg4: Stop Loss / Target Reached — Sent when an open position hits its stop loss or target price. Shows entry, current price, and P&L.
howItWorks.tgMsg5: Signal Weakening — Sent when the signal for an open position changes status (e.g. CONFIRMED → WEAKENING). Helps you decide whether to hold or exit.
howItWorks.tgMsg6: OTP / Brain Code — One-time codes for login and Brain Editor access. Valid for 2 minutes (login) or 60 seconds (brain).
howItWorks.tgMsgNote: Messages are bilingual (EN/PT) based on your language setting. Tickers that did not qualify for AI analysis show a technical-only summary instead of AI reasoning.
howItWorks.readingDetailTitle: How to Read a Signal Detail Page
howItWorks.readingDetailDesc: When you tap a ticker from the signals list, you see its full analysis. Here is how to interpret each section:
howItWorks.readingDetail1: Score Ring (0-100) — The overall signal strength. Safe Income needs 62+ for BUY, High Risk needs 65+. Above 85 with other conditions = GEM.
howItWorks.readingDetail2: Sub-score pills — Colored labels beneath the score showing what's driving the signal: 'Dividend Reliability: Strong', 'Macro: Neutral'. Green = contributing strongly, red = dragging the score down, gray = neutral.
howItWorks.readingDetail3: Probability vs SPY — A badge showing the chance of beating the S&P 500 in 20 days (e.g., '64% chance of beating SPY'). Based on backtest data for this score range and bucket type.
howItWorks.readingDetail4: Target Price / Stop Loss / Risk Reward — The AI-estimated exit points. Compare target with current price for upside. Stop loss limits downside. R/R above 2x is good, above 3x is excellent.
howItWorks.readingDetail5: Confidence + Market Regime — Confidence shows how sure the AI is (50%+ required for BUY). Regime badge shows TRENDING (normal), VOLATILE (caution), or CRISIS (defensive only).
howItWorks.readingDetail6: Why This Signal — The AI reasoning from Claude explaining what technical indicators, fundamentals, and market conditions drove the recommendation. Tech-only signals show 'AI skipped' when the ticker didn't make the top 15.
howItWorks.readingDetail7: Score Breakdown — Progress bars showing how the score is weighted. Safe Income: 35% dividends, 30% fundamentals, 25% macro, 10% sentiment. High Risk: 35% sentiment, 30% catalyst, 25% momentum, 10% fundamentals.
howItWorks.readingDetail8: Fundamentals — Real data from Yahoo Finance: P/E ratio, EPS growth, dividend yield, market cap, profit margin, debt/equity, beta, and short interest (% of shares sold short).
howItWorks.readingDetail9: Period changes — 1W, 1M, 3M, and YTD returns compared against SPY. Green if outperforming the benchmark, helps assess relative strength.
howItWorks.readingDetail10: Signa Brain Says — AI-generated insights specific to this ticker, including key points (positive, warning, or info), relevant brain knowledge, and the historical track record for this score range.
howItWorks.readingDetail11: Signal History — Previous signals for this ticker so you can see if the recommendation has been consistent or changing.
howItWorks.readingDetailExample: Example: ENB.TO at score 69 with 'Dividend Reliability: Strong' and 'Macro: Neutral' pills. The probability chip shows '64% vs SPY'. Brain says it's a solid Safe Income play with 4.2% dividend yield and low debt. Short interest is only 1.2% — no squeeze risk. The fundamentals are strong but macro conditions (VIX elevated) are keeping the score below BUY threshold.
howItWorks.virtualPortfolioDesc: Signa automatically tracks what would happen if you followed every signal the brain generates for your watchlisted tickers. No manual input needed — the brain proves its own accuracy.
howItWorks.vp1: When a watchlisted ticker gets a BUY signal (score 62+), the brain "virtually buys" at the current price.
howItWorks.vp2: When that same ticker later gets a SELL or AVOID signal, the brain "virtually sells" and calculates the profit or loss.
howItWorks.vp3: Win Rate = percentage of virtual trades that were profitable. This is the brain's real accuracy metric.
howItWorks.vp4: Average Return = mean P&L% across all closed virtual trades. Positive means the brain is generating alpha.
howItWorks.vp5: Open virtual positions are shown on the dashboard — these are tickers the brain currently "holds".
howItWorks.vp6: The more tickers in your watchlist, the more data the brain collects, and the more accurate the win rate becomes.
howItWorks.vpNote: Virtual trades are paper trades only — no real money is involved. They exist purely to measure the brain's signal quality over time. After enough data (20+ closed trades), the self-learning loop can analyze patterns and suggest rule improvements.
howItWorks.watchdogDesc: The 4 daily scans discover new signals, but what about the positions the brain already holds? The Brain Watchdog monitors all open brain positions every 15 minutes during market hours, acting as a real-time safety net.
howItWorks.wd1: Every 15 minutes, the watchdog checks all open brain positions. It triggers on: interval drops >= 2%, total loss >= 2%, proximity to stop loss, score deterioration (10+ points), or composite concern (weakest position + losing + held 1+ day).
howItWorks.wd2: On escalation, the watchdog runs a quick Grok sentiment check and schedules a 5-minute follow-up for faster monitoring. If bearish sentiment + negative P&L, the brain auto-sells. If neutral/bullish, it holds and keeps rechecking every 5 minutes until the position recovers or is closed.
howItWorks.wd3: Emergency exits happen immediately without sentiment checks: total loss exceeding -8%, score collapsing below 50, or a SELL/AVOID signal on a losing position. These are force-sold to prevent catastrophic losses. You always get a Telegram notification for these, even during quiet hours.
howItWorks.wd4: Profit-taking: when a position reaches +3% after being held for 2+ days, the brain locks in the gain. Unrealized profits aren't real money -- the brain converts them to realized returns to hit the 1-2% monthly target.
howItWorks.wd5: After 3 consecutive hold-through-dip events with bullish sentiment on the same ticker, the watchdog enters a 1-hour cooldown to reduce alert noise. After-hours, monitoring continues at 60-minute intervals. Weekends: if 'Weekend Crypto Watchdog' is enabled in Settings, the brain monitors crypto positions hourly (crypto trades 24/7). Equities are skipped since prices don't change on weekends.
howItWorks.wd6: Every watchdog decision (alert, close, hold, recovery, force-sell, profit-take) is logged to the database with timestamps and Yahoo Finance links. The self-learning loop analyzes these weekly to improve the brain's rules.
howItWorks.wdNote: The watchdog uses Grok sentiment ($0.0002/call) and Gemini (free) only when concerned — typically 2-3 sentiment checks per day. Total monthly cost is roughly $0.03. Price checks via Yahoo Finance are always free.
howItWorks.wdCooldownDesc: If the watchdog checks the same ticker 3 times in a row and keeps holding, it enters a 1-hour cooldown to reduce alert noise. You'll still see all events logged, but you won't get spammed with Telegram messages for a ticker that's hovering near a threshold without actually breaking down.
howItWorks.weekendCryptoDesc: Crypto markets trade 24/7, including weekends. When enabled in Settings, the brain monitors crypto positions every hour on Saturdays and Sundays. If a crypto position hits a stop, drops more than 2%, or shows bearish sentiment, the brain will act -- just like during the week. Equity positions are skipped on weekends since stock markets are closed. This is off by default to save on API costs, but recommended if you hold crypto positions over the weekend.
howItWorks.fearGreedDesc: The dashboard shows the CNN Fear & Greed Index (0-100) alongside VIX and other macro data. Extreme Fear periods are historically better for buying, while Extreme Greed suggests caution. The AI providers also receive this data so their analysis reflects current market psychology.
howItWorks.shortInterestDesc: Each ticker's short interest (% of shares sold short) is shown on the signal detail page under Fundamentals. High short interest combined with bullish momentum can trigger squeeze potential, which the scoring system rewards with bonus points. Look for this when evaluating HIGH_RISK signals.
howItWorks.probVsSpyDesc: Every signal card shows a probability chip like '62% vs SPY' — this tells you how likely the signal is to beat the S&P 500 over 20 days, based on historical backtest data for that score range. Higher scores and AI-analyzed signals have better odds. Use this to compare signals at a glance.
howItWorks.factorLabelsDesc: On the signal detail page, colored pills beneath the score show what's driving the signal: 'Dividend Reliability: Strong', 'Macro: Neutral', 'Sentiment: Weak'. Green means that factor is contributing strongly to the score. Red means it's dragging it down. This helps you understand why a signal scored the way it did.
howItWorks.cryptoScalingDesc: Crypto positions are sized more conservatively than equities — half the normal Kelly position size and a tighter 8% maximum stop loss. This reflects crypto's higher volatility. You'll see this reflected in the Kelly recommendation on crypto signal cards.
howItWorks.trackRecordDesc: The brain performance page shows historical win rates by score range: 'When brain gave BUY at 75+, win rate was X%'. This is split between brain auto-picks and your watchlist picks so you can compare. The signal detail page also shows the relevant track record for each ticker's current score range.
howItWorks.brainRadarDesc: The sidebar shows a 'Brain Radar' section with tickers the brain would pick right now -- BUY signals scoring 72+ from the latest scan, up to 3 stocks and 3 crypto. This is different from 'Brain Auto-Picks' in the performance widget, which shows positions the brain actually holds. Think of Radar as 'what the brain sees' and Auto-Picks as 'what the brain owns'.
howItWorks.budgetSystemDesc: Signa tracks every AI API call and enforces spending limits to prevent surprise bills. Each provider has a configurable monthly budget.
howItWorks.budget1: Claude (synthesis) — costs ~/bin/zsh.012 per call. Used for the top 15 candidates per scan to generate signal reasoning, target prices, and risk analysis.
howItWorks.budget2: Grok (sentiment) — costs ~/bin/zsh.008 per call. Used for HIGH_RISK candidates to analyze real-time X/Twitter sentiment.
howItWorks.budget3: Gemini (fallback) — free tier, 1,500 requests/day. Used when Claude or Grok are unavailable or over budget.
howItWorks.budget4: Daily limit — maximum spend per provider per day (default .00). Prevents a single runaway scan from draining your credits.
howItWorks.budget5: Monthly limit — total cap per provider per month (default .00 each for Claude and Grok). When reached, the provider is skipped and the fallback chain kicks in.
howItWorks.budget6: At ~/bin/zsh.26 per scan, you can run about 19 full scans on  per provider. With 4 daily auto-scans, that's roughly 4-5 days of coverage.
howItWorks.budgetNote: Budget limits are configurable on the Integrations page. The budget card on the dashboard shows real-time spend. When a provider hits its limit, signals still generate using tech-only scoring (no AI cost).
howItWorks.notificationsDesc: Signa sends Telegram alerts automatically during scans. You do not need to do anything — alerts are triggered by the scan pipeline based on your watchlist and signal conditions.
howItWorks.notif1: GEM Alerts are sent instantly when a signal meets all 5 GEM conditions during a scan. These are rare and highly actionable.
howItWorks.notif2: Watchlist Alerts are sent when any ticker on your watchlist receives a SELL or AVOID signal. Add tickers to your watchlist to receive these.
howItWorks.notif3: Scan Digests are sent after the Pre-Market (6 AM) and After-Close (4:30 PM) scans with a summary of top signals.
howItWorks.notif4: Position Alerts (stop loss hit, target reached, signal weakening) are sent when you have open positions being monitored.
howItWorks.notif5: All alerts appear in the Recent Alerts card on the dashboard and in your Telegram chat with the Signa bot.
howItWorks.notif6: To test if Telegram is working, go to the Integrations page and click Send Test Message.
howItWorks.notifNote: Alerts are only generated during scans. Scans run 4 times per day Mon-Fri (6 AM, 10 AM, 3 PM, 4:30 PM ET). No scans or alerts on weekends.

settings.title: Settings
settings.aiAnalysisOnDesc: Top candidates get AI reasoning + sentiment
settings.dragHint: Drag providers to set priority. The first available provider is used; others are fallbacks.
settings.preFilterDesc: Pre-filter selects top {max} by volume/price action. {aiDesc}
settings.aiOnDesc: Top {limit} get AI analysis, rest get tech-only scoring.
settings.aiOffDesc: All candidates get tech-only scoring (no AI cost).
settings.backtestValidated: Backtest validated: Safe Income at {threshold}+ has {count} signals over 2 years
settings.highRiskNeedsAi: High Risk needs AI sentiment for better accuracy. Scorer alone maxes at ~55%.
settings.belowScoreAvoid: Below this score = AVOID. Above = HOLD (waiting for confirmation).
settings.minNotifyDesc: Moves smaller than this are logged but won't send Telegram notifications.
settings.pnlAlertDesc: Triggers concern when a position drops this much in a single 15-min check.
settings.maxBrainDesc: Maximum concurrent brain auto-picks. When full, the brain rotates out weaker positions for stronger signals.
settings.weekendCryptoDesc: Monitor crypto positions on weekends (hourly). Crypto trades 24/7.
settings.subtitle: AI providers, scanning, thresholds, themes, and language.
settings.confirmSaveMessage: Are you sure? This will update your signal configuration.
settings.quietHoursDesc: Block Telegram notifications during these hours.

brain.otpSent: A 6-digit code has been sent to your Telegram. It expires in 60 seconds.
brain.readonlyNote: name, rule_type, bucket, and source cannot be changed here.
brain.knowledgeReadonlyNote: topic, key_concept, and source cannot be changed here.
brain.suggestionsDesc: AI analyzes your trade outcomes and suggests brain improvements.
brain.noSuggestions: No suggestions yet. Record some trades and run an analysis.
brain.wfFullSignals: Full signal generation. Normal Kelly sizing.
brain.wfReducedSignals: High Risk scores reduced 15%. Kelly halved.
brain.wfPausedSignals: High Risk paused. Safe Income dividends only. Kelly capped 5%.
brain.wfUniverseDetail: Scans TSX (.TO), NYSE, NASDAQ, and 18 crypto tickers (-USD). Includes banks, energy, tech, REITs, ETFs, cannabis, and more.
brain.wfPrefilterDetail: Filters by volume (min 200K), price change (min 1%), and price (min $1). Reserves 5 slots for crypto. Configurable in Settings.
brain.wfRegimeDetail: Runs ONCE per scan. VIX < 20 = TRENDING (normal). VIX 20-30 = VOLATILE (cautious). VIX > 30 = CRISIS (defensive, High Risk paused).
brain.wfTechnicalsDetail: RSI(14), MACD(12,26,9), Bollinger Bands(20,2), SMA 50/200, volume z-score, momentum 5d/20d, ATR. Sweet spot: RSI 50-65.
brain.wfFundamentalsDetail: P/E ratio, EPS growth, dividend yield, payout ratio, debt/equity, profit margin, revenue growth, market cap, sector.
brain.wfMacroDetail: Fed funds rate, 10Y treasury, CPI inflation, unemployment, VIX. Hostile macro auto-blocks BUY signals.
brain.wfSentimentDetail: X/Twitter sentiment via Grok (real-time) or Gemini (fallback), enriched with options flow data (put/call ratio, IV percentile, unusual volume). Score 0-100. When sentiment and options agree, confidence is boosted. Hidden if confidence=0. Skipped for Safe Income (10% weight).
brain.wfSynthesisDetail: All data sent to Claude/Gemini with brain knowledge context. Returns: action, confidence, reasoning, target, stop loss, risk/reward.
brain.wfScoringDetail: Bucket-specific weights. Safe Income: dividends 35%, fundamentals 30%, macro 25%, sentiment 10%. High Risk: sentiment 35%, catalyst 30%, technical 25%, fundamentals 10%.
brain.wfBlockersDetail: 8 auto-AVOID blockers: RSI>75, hostile macro, low volume, fraud keywords, price<$1, VIX crisis. Override score regardless.
brain.wfActionDetail: Score 75-90 = BUY. Score > 90 = HOLD (overbought ceiling). Score 50-74 = HOLD. Score < 50 = AVOID.
brain.wfGemDetail: ALL 5 required: score >= 85, bullish sentiment >= 80% confidence, catalyst within 30 days, R/R >= 3.0, zero red flags. Instant Telegram alert.
brain.wfKellyDetail: Fractional Kelly (25%). Maps score to win rate. Max 15% position. VOLATILE halves sizing. CRISIS caps at 5%.
brain.wfSignalDetail: Persisted to Supabase with all metadata. Enriched with current price on read. Account recommendation (TFSA/RRSP) attached.
brain.wfAlertsDetail: GEM alerts instant. Watchlist SELL/AVOID alerts instant. Scan digests for PRE_MARKET and AFTER_CLOSE. Bilingual (EN/PT).

logs.otpSent: A 6-digit code has been sent to your Telegram.
logs.requiresElevated: Real-time application logs require elevated access.
logs.subtitle: Real-time scan activity and system events.

brainPerf.performanceSubtitle: Virtual portfolio tracking — proving the brain's accuracy with real market data.
brainPerf.noTradesYet: No closed trades yet. The brain is holding virtual positions — results will appear when signals flip to SELL or AVOID.
brainPerf.trackRecordDesc: Win rates and average returns segmented by entry score range
